layers_note: Two-layer bible: sector_allocation is the most recent monthly layer (2026-06-11). Core stock ideas (top5/bottom5/what_to_own, source_file) remain from the 2026-05-28 deck - no newer stock-ideas deck has been published.

sector_allocation.source: Fundstrat June 2026 Sector Allocation Update (email 2026-06-11 09:10 ET); full distill archived in Notion FS Inbox archive

newton_rating_changes.why: RSPH/RSP breakout above 5-month downtrend, TD Combo 13 confirmed 6/4; XHS at new all-time highs; strong Jun-Aug seasonality, strength may last into fall

Consolidation of overbought momentum, NOT a top - weekly RSI ~87.8, TD 13 sell on RSPT/RSP, semis -10% single session early June; expects Tech to struggle to outperform until the midterms; stretched names need weeks of base-building

Loses leadership spot to Health Care/Utilities/REITs - not a bearish call; XLI/SPY at multi-year trendline support with weekly TD Buy Setup near completion; Tom keeps his Overweight, Industrials still among his top 2026 picks

agreement.note: Shared Lee/Newton ratings narrowed to 3; divergence reflects different time horizons, not disagreement on market direction

june_etf_basket.theme: cybersecurity - best-performing software segment in rebound; least AI-disrupted part of Tech
healthcare providers - direct expression of the Health Care upgrade post-XHS breakout
genomics - higher-upside Health Care expression without IHF's managed-care overlap
transportation - top-ranked composite ETF; direct beneficiary of falling crude / ceasefire progress
autonomous/EV large-cap basket (NVDA, GOOGL, INTC, QCOM, TSLA, MSFT); leveraged automation exposure

sector_allocation.may_basket_grade: Loss: -5.0% absolute, -3.7% vs SPX since 5/6 (best SNSR +4.8% rel; worst LIT -15.0% rel). Already graded in Source Call Log - do not re-log.

tactical_top3.why: Strategic upgrade plus strongest short-term momentum; up 7.1% over the prior 30 days, Trend Score moved from #10 to #3, and seasonality supports June-August relative strength.
Carry-over top-3 sector and largest benchmark overweight; Trend Score #1 and broad member participation support adding on pullbacks ahead of a REIT breakout.
Expected to outperform as yields fall, with crude and long-term yields late in their bounces and ceasefire progress as a possible catalyst.

tactical_bottom3.why: Third consecutive tactical bottom-3 month; Newton overrides strong backward-looking quant ranks because a more concrete ceasefire and Strait reopening could flush crude lower.
New bottom-3 sector after the weakest May-to-June performance in coverage; travel/services demand and EPS revisions remain soft despite an XHB sub-sector offset.
Defensive laggard with weaker risk/reward than Health Care, Utilities, or REITs; remains the largest benchmark underweight.

named_levels.why: A weekly close above the $38 area would argue for an upside resolution of the multi-year symmetrical triangle.

monthly_checklist.decision_use: Use tactical 3x3 as timing color through the next monthly sector allocation update, then supersede it.
Lean Health Care, Real Estate, and Utilities over Energy, Consumer Discretionary, and Staples when comparing sector ETF adds.
Treat Tech as trimmed from double-overweight, not a bearish thesis break; stretched names need base-building before full-size adds.
Weekly close above $38 confirms the REIT breakout layer and can upgrade REIT/Real Estate timing.
CIBR, IHF, ARKG, IYT, and DRIV are the current 3% tactical ETF basket names; deleted May ETFs should not be treated as active Fundstrat adds.

compact_backfill.absolute_add_prices: not provided in source email; keep add_price null until approved historical close cache backfills it
